findings.proposition: random_lottery_component accepts lottery_share_bps=-1000; pool=100 with a well-formed sole donor yields total issuance 110 and scenario unissued_remainder=-10, violating the module's documented supply invariant.
findings.supporting_evidence: Independent tribunal re-implementation confirms issuance=110, remainder=-10 at function and scenario level.
findings.counterevidence: None; committed scenario corpus uses only valid values, so committed results reproduce (72/72, 25/25).
findings.repair: Boundary-validate lottery_share_bps (exact int, non-bool, 0..10000); assert 0<=total_issued<=pool before return and scenario emission.
findings.regression_test: Direct+scenario tests for -1000, 0, 10000, 10001, True, 250.5, '10'; expect ParticipantValidationError and invariant gate.

findings.proposition: Scheme-parameter domain is systematically unvalidated in the REDTEAM package: bool bps accepted; >10000/float/string rejected only accidentally; negative early_bonus_bps inverts documented semantics; inverted epoch bounds accepted; rng unvalidated duck typing.
findings.supporting_evidence: Probe outcomes: T-003 FAIL_OPEN (True as 1bps), T-006 FAIL_OPEN_SEMANTIC_INVERSION, T-007 FAIL_OPEN, T-008 FAIL_OPEN; T-002/T-004/T-005 FAIL_CLOSED_ACCIDENTAL.
findings.counterevidence: Supply invariant held in T-006/T-007 cases; only TRIB-F-001's case breaches it.
findings.repair: Shared scheme-parameter validator covering every bps/bound parameter, epoch ordering, and rng contract.
findings.regression_test: Same adversarial value set per parameter; semantic ordering test for time_weighted.

findings.proposition: Design README states 29 executable/6 documentary/8 residual; gate and EXPECTED.json yield 34/1/8; 36 files in fixtures/invalid with the 36th (duplicate_raw_claim) exercised by a separate raw-parser gate check.
tribunal_probes: gate rerun exit 0: executed=34 documentary=1 residual=8
findings.supporting_evidence: PR42 and PR44 receipts record identical gate output.
findings.counterevidence: Gate itself is internally consistent; drift is README-only.
findings.repair: Update README to 34/1/8 plus separately-exercised raw-parse fixture note, or generate counts from EXPECTED.json.
findings.regression_test: Gate check asserting README counts match EXPECTED.json.

findings.proposition: Full lab tests (9F+2E) and ./nexus verify fail on a clean tree at missing @noble/ed25519; npm ci fully resolves (185/185, verify PASS); dependency correctly pinned in package.json/lock; prerequisite undocumented in root docs and receipts.
tribunal_probes: before/after npm ci runs this session
findings.supporting_evidence: All four seats plus tribunal observe identical failure signature and resolution.
findings.repair: Document npm ci prerequisite in root README/AUDIT_START_HERE and receipt environment fields; always state working directory for prescribed commands (RETEST_002 node --test is cwd-sensitive).
findings.regression_test: Clean-clone CI job: npm ci && full suite && nexus verify.

findings.proposition: PR42's single handoff label TECHNICAL_EVIDENCE_HOLD_FOR_REPAIR is accurate in substance but not aligned to the three-category machine vocabulary; mapped: TECHNICAL_PACKAGE=REPAIR_REQUIRED, CLAIM_EVIDENCE=OVERSTATED (for the unqualified supply-invariant claim), REAL_WORLD_READINESS=RESEARCH_ONLY.
findings.source_locator: PR42 RECEIPT.json decision field; issue #41 (no vocabulary prescribed); PR43 FINAL_VERDICT.json allowed_values
findings.supporting_evidence: Receipt separately records economic_gate_pass:false and mechanism non-adjudication; operative consequence identical.
findings.counterevidence: Task did not prescribe the vocabulary, so no violation occurred.
findings.repair: Prescribe exact verdict vocabulary in future audit tasks.

findings.proposition: PR43's TECHNICAL_PACKAGE=PASS_WITH_STATED_LIMITS rested on a search that excluded the scheme-parameter domain; its package-wide 'type strictness / bool-as-int holds' non-findings are overclaimed; its own F-004 located the unregistered surface without probing it.
findings.source_locator: PR43 probes/run_probes.py (lottery_share_bps only in {1000,5000,10000}); FINDINGS.md non-findings table
findings.supporting_evidence: TRIB-F-001/TRIB-F-002 instances fall inside the package public surface and outside PR43's search.
findings.counterevidence: PR43's four explicit findings (F-001..F-004) are all correct and confirmed; its probes were sound on their domain.
findings.repair: Treat single-seat passes as bounded to the searched domain; require search-dimension declaration in audit reports.

findings.proposition: Several invalid fixtures map to broader rejection codes than their names imply while more specific codes exist; all still fail closed.
findings.reproduced_by_tribunal: partially (mappings inspected; per-fixture codes not re-traced beyond gate run)
findings.repair: Prefer specific codes; test code stability; document intentional overloads.

findings.proposition: SCHEMES registry omits the lottery scheme; the scenario runner special-cases it; the unregistered special-case surface is where the validation gap survived three reviews.
findings.supporting_evidence: Source reads; TRIB-F-001 lived exactly on this surface.
findings.repair: Registry entry with explicit rng-factory contract or documented special-case with equal boundary validation.

findings.proposition: 43/0/0 differential agreement (tribunal rerun exact) proves cross-implementation parity on the committed fixture corpus only; not input-domain completeness, not real-chain semantics.
findings.supporting_evidence: Exact agreement figures reproduced this session.
findings.counterevidence: TRIB-F-001 demonstrates a defect outside every fixture corpus.

findings.proposition: Participation elasticity (additional vs displaced giving) is unmodelled in every artifact; donation volume is exogenous in all simulators; it is the sign-controlling variable for welfare claims.
findings.source_locator: All subject model population/scenario modules take donors as fixed input; PR44 FINAL_VERDICT EVIDENCE_STATE; PR45 SUMMARY.json key_unresolved_empirical
findings.independence_note: PR44/PR45 rediscovery under declared blindness to PR40; shared operator task wording not in repository, so prompt-seeded convergence cannot be fully excluded.
findings.supporting_evidence: No participation-response module exists anywhere in the subject.
findings.repair: Preregistered empirical participation/flow-composition research design (Phase 5 B).

findings.proposition: Transferability necessity NOT_DEMONSTRATED: every specified ledger function (receipt binding, charitable transfer binding, finite allocation, permissionless claim) is implementable without transferable units; functions that could require it are unspecified and untested.
findings.source_locator: PR44 FINAL_VERDICT TRANSFERABILITY_NECESSITY; PR45 results transferability_necessity_structural; design pack claim objects (no alienability requirement)
findings.supporting_evidence: Two structurally different independent models reach it by construction; design pack verifier nowhere requires alienability.
findings.counterevidence: Unspecified future functions (payment, fees, staking, bonding) might require transfer; untested.
findings.confidence: HIGH within specified functions; LOW for unspecified future ledger
findings.repair: Product/ledger function specification with acceptance table (Phase 5 B).
findings.regression_test: Executable required-function acceptance test per function; falsifier is one only a transferable design passes.

findings.proposition: Identity-free anti-concentration fails: linear pro-rata split-invariant; concave weighting strictly Sybil-rewarding; per-identity caps evaded by splitting; cap-then-renormalize not a hard cap (holder can end above nominal cap, up to 100%).
findings.source_locator: PR45 results 03/04; PR44 CLAIM_LEDGER C2-C4; subject results 05/06/26 and 14_governance_capped
findings.supporting_evidence: Three independent implementations agree; deduction elementary.
findings.counterevidence: An enforceable permissionless actor boundary would defeat the countermodels; none known without identity.
findings.repair: Identity stance must be specified; concave/cap schemes must not be advertised as identity-free fairness.

findings.proposition: PR44 EVIDENCE_STATE=UNDERDETERMINED and PR45 EVIDENCE_STATE=SUFFICIENT_FOR_BOUNDED_DECISION answer different questions (final mechanism choice vs bounded research decision) and are compatible; each seat's own qualifications endorse the other's answer on the other's question.
findings.source_locator: PR44 FINAL_VERDICT.md EVIDENCE_STATE section; PR45 FINAL_VERDICT.json EVIDENCE_STATE assumptions/counterevidence
findings.supporting_evidence: Exact verdict texts: PR45 assumes 'Bounded decision is research disposition not launch' and concedes empirical variables open; PR44's falsifier is empirical calibration closing sign-controlling variables.
findings.repair: Record both statements with their objects; do not report a mechanism-audit contradiction.

findings.proposition: Sole minimum-positive-contribution donor captures the entire fixed pool (confirmed in REDTEAM model and normative design pack); extreme oversubscription floors 1-sat donors to zero; fixed-pool issuance has unbounded per-unit payout at the undersubscribed extreme.
findings.supporting_evidence: T-013: 1 sat -> 10^9 units in both implementations; T-014: 100/100 small donors floored to zero, supply conserved.
findings.counterevidence: The rule does what it documents; this is a design consequence, not a bug.
findings.repair: POLICY_CHOICE in specification: fixed-pool vs flow-linked issuance, minimum-participation floor.

findings.proposition: Attaching majority governance to raw allocation is unsafe under every examined weighting; governance-from-allocation guarantees degrade after transfer; both mechanism audits condition continuation on governance separation.
findings.supporting_evidence: prop_top_share 4/5; capped sybil 9/10; coalition splitting increases power.
findings.counterevidence: Governance coupling is currently a deferred POLICY_CHOICE, not implemented.
findings.repair: Specification must state governance separation or durable constraints.

findings.proposition: Rebate capture, tainted-flow, and whale-collusion pathways exist and are conditionally profitable; profitability flips sign with assumed token value/access/retention; incidence, magnitude, and inevitability are unestablished in either direction; combined attacks modeled only in limited grids.
findings.source_locator: PR45 SUMMARY.json collusion_conditional (sign flip across v grid), tainted 26/60 rows; PR44 CLAIM_LEDGER C7
findings.supporting_evidence: Grids committed with exact parameters; consistent across independent implementations.
findings.counterevidence: No empirical calibration; pathway existence is not incidence.
findings.confidence: HIGH for pathway existence; UNRESOLVED for magnitude
findings.repair: Empirical research design; charity-curation control design (Track D).
findings.regression_test: Keep committed grids; add combined multi-variable attack grids in next mechanism round.

findings.proposition: The record's controlling adjudications lack receipts/seat identity/method statements; the pre-audit disposition was prescribed before repair/retest; the five fresh audit PRs partially remedy this by arriving at CONTINUE_WITH_CONDITIONS from fresh sessions.
findings.reproduced_by_tribunal: structural facts yes; timing claims not re-derived
findings.supporting_evidence: Absence of receipts for adjudications is checkable in the tree.
findings.counterevidence: Adjudication content held up under PR40's re-verification.
findings.repair: Receipted adjudications with seat identity and freeze for all future controlling reviews.

findings.proposition: Provider/model independence across the five audits is self-declared (one GitHub account performs all actions; labels are branch names and receipt strings); the record must not be described as 'five independent AIs agreed'.
findings.source_locator: git author fields; receipts provider_model_label strings; no attestation artifacts
findings.supporting_evidence: Divergent findings (PR42 vs PR43) and distinct code styles weakly suggest distinct generating processes but prove nothing about provider identity.
findings.repair: Per-seat signed attestations or separately controlled accounts if independence claims are needed.
